channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: U.S. renews strikes on Iran, citing threats posed to American troops
item.dc:creator: Dan Lamothe, John Hudson
item.description: The new U.S. military action threatens to complicate a shaky ceasefire and ongoing negotiations to end a months-long war.

item.title: As new Iran deal faces sharp criticism, Trump seeks to widen Abraham Accords
item.dc:creator: Sammy Westfall, John Hudson
item.description: GOP lawmakers warned against concessions to Iran in any deal to end the war, while doubts greeted Trump’s push to expand pacts between Israel and Muslim-majority nations.

item.title: Russia’s overnight barrage on Ukraine includes use of powerful Oreshnik missile
item.dc:creator: David Stern, Kostiantyn Khudov, Catherine Belton
item.description: At least two people died in Kyiv, which faced the brunt of the attack, Ukrainian officials said. It was one of the biggest overnight aerial assaults since the beginning of Russia's invasion.

item.title: Africa is Catholicism’s future, but Pentecostal churches are growing faster
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason, Anthony Faiola
item.description: The growth of Christianity in Africa has turned the continent into a denominational battleground. Pope Leo’s visit to Angola came as his church confronts a rising challenge.
